structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections, labelled assumptions, locked-parameter block, ExactID identifying functional, target estimand, proof section, sanity checks, minimality discussion, and math-object checklist are present and usable downstream. The locked block is cluster-consistent overall, although it conflates causal identifying assumptions with inference-frontier regularity.

novelty.notes: The note is below the flagship floor. The named object \(\mathfrak F_n(P)\) is clear, but the claimed necessary-and-sufficient frontier is obtained only after imposing high-level sharp upper and lower frontier assumptions rather than deriving them from primitive Holder/entropy conditions or from named published structural restrictions.

correctness.notes: The AIPW identification algebra and Holder exponent calculation are mostly correct, but the headline iff theorem is largely a consequence of Assumptions \ref{ass:crossfit} and \ref{ass:curvature}, which already encode the upper-bound Gaussian channel and active lower-bound failures at the same frontier scale.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:frontier} and Assumptions \ref{ass:crossfit}, \ref{ass:curvature}
perItemFindings.one_line: The theorem claims an iff frontier, but the oracle Gaussian upper channel, sharp empirical-process scale, and minimax lower channels are assumed at lines 292-313 and 329-351 rather than proved from primitives.

perItemFindings.label: Novelty positioning, lines 158-179 and 418-446
perItemFindings.one_line: The closest-literature comparison says prior work lacks this exact frontier, but the note does not prove the missing frontier mechanism; it conditions on a free active-frontier assumption, so the current derivation is at most field-tier and below novelty_target=flagship.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:holder-frontier}
perItemFindings.one_line: The Holder-rate theorem is an algebraic specialization of \(\mathfrak F_n\) once the rates and active-channel experiment are assumed; it is not an independent flagship contribution.

perItemFindings.label: Oracle-process centering in Assumption \ref{ass:entropy} and Theorem \ref{thm:score-remainder}
perItemFindings.one_line: The process is centered as \(\ell(X)\{\Gamma-\theta_0(\ell)\}\), which requires the additional displayed convention at lines 276-287; without that convention the natural influence function for \(E[\ell\Gamma]\) is \(\ell\Gamma-\theta_0(\ell)\).

perItemFindings.label: Related work and flagship comparison
perItemFindings.one_line: The note names DML, CATE, Gaussian approximation, and limitations references, but does not identify a published open regime or derive a new primitive maximal inequality/lower-bound construction separating it from those comparators.

verbatim_critique: The note has the Stage 0 skeleton in usable shape and the ExactID AIPW identity is correctly separated from the causal target before equality is proved. The main issue is that the flagship frontier is not actually derived. In the Main theorems section, Theorem \ref{thm:frontier} asserts that first-order DML is uniformly Gaussian if and only if \(\sup_P\mathfrak F_n(P)\to0\), but the decisive content is already built into Assumption \ref{ass:crossfit} and Assumption \ref{ass:curvature}. Lines 292-313 assume the oracle Gaussian route and the nuisance empirical-process upper scale. Lines 329-351 assume active tangent directions and lower bounds at exactly the drift and empirical-process scales. The proof at lines 557-665 then combines those assumptions with a Cauchy--Schwarz bilinear bound. That is mathematically coherent as a conditional theorem, but it is not a proof of the promised sharp Holder/entropy DML frontier from primitive conditions. Theorem \ref{thm:holder-frontier} is also correct only as an algebraic specialization of the already-assumed frontier: lines 667-792 compute the exponents and then reuse the same active-channel assumptions for failure below the boundary. This does not clear the flagship novelty floor because the high-level active-frontier assumption is not a published named structural restriction and effectively imports the claimed boundary. To repair the derivation, Stage 0 should either downgrade the claim to a conditional field-level theorem or replace the free frontier assumptions with primitive upper and lower arguments: an explicit entropy/maximal inequality for \(\Delta_n^{\mathrm{ep}}\), a construction of active Holder nuisance perturbations attaining \(R_P\), and a genuine lower-bound or contiguity/minimax experiment showing failure when either channel is order one. The related-work section also needs a sharper theorem-level comparison to the closest DML/CATE and orthogonal-learning results, explaining which primitive regime those papers leave open and how the new proof resolves it. Finally, the oracle-process centering convention at lines 276-287 should be made explicit in the theorem statement or replaced by the natural influence function \(\ell(X)\Gamma(W;\eta_0)-\theta_0(\ell)\), because the current centered process differs unless the displayed convention is imposed.
